_doc: SURFACE PARITY REGISTRY. This contract exists because zzop shipped the same bug class more than once: the facade computed a field (configWarnings; git-derived health/recommendations/critical; the run-level, join-scoped MultiAnalyzeOutput::warnings channel, e.g. the parallel-implementation tripwire) but the delivery surface silently dropped it. Every top-level field of AnalyzeOutputView and MultiAnalyzeOutputView (crates/facade/src/output.rs) must get exactly one row below, naming what the delivery surface — the MCP analyze_repo/cross_repo reply, which is the SAME shaped summary the `zzop-mcp analyze`/`cross` CLI subcommands print — does with it: 'carry' (forwarded unconditionally), 'carry-conditional' (forwarded only in some shaped/capped/gated form, or only when a precondition holds), or 'omit' (never surfaced by the reply). Every 'omit'/'carry-conditional' row MUST carry a non-empty 'note' explaining why and where the data IS available — which, for anything the shaped reply drops, is the raw zzop-facade output you get by embedding the engine directly (no shipped CLI/MCP surface emits the full CommonIr). HISTORICAL NOTE: this registry briefly tracked two JS-CLI render surfaces (a pretty terminal render backed by lib/format.js and a markdown report backed by lib/report.js) across the npm distribution's 2026-07-20 removal-then-restoration; @zzop/cli is now a zero-logic shim that spawns the native `zzop` binary with no render logic of its own, so those two columns are dropped again and only the reply lane remains. WORKFLOW FOR FUTURE BATCH AGENTS: adding a field to AnalyzeOutputView or MultiAnalyzeOutputView? Add this registry's row for it IN THE SAME COMMIT — crates/engine/tests/rule_contracts/surface_parity.rs's completeness test fails the build otherwise, by design. This file documents TODAY's truth, not an aspiration: a row reflects what the code actually does, not what it should eventually do.

cache.note: Internal engine cache-hit/miss diagnostic (CacheStatsView). Not referenced anywhere in crates/host/src or crates/summary/src. Available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding) — a deliberate internal-only performance diagnostic, not part of any curated summary.

critical.note: Only the top 3 paths (engine's own blast-radius ranking) ride the compact `architecture.criticalTop` summary (crates/summary/src/analyze/mod.rs::architecture_summary); the full CriticalFile list (with contributing metrics) is available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

degraded.note: The raw degraded-path list is capped (DEFAULT_DEGRADED_LIMIT=50) with an explicit `degradedTruncated` disclosure when capped (crates/summary/src/analyze/mod.rs) — `coverage.degraded` already carries the full uncapped count. The full uncapped per-file list is available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

findings.note: Shaped via output::shape_findings into {total, bySeverity, byRule, shown (severity-desc, capped at DEFAULT_FINDINGS_LIMIT=50 unless the caller supplies `limit`), truncated?} — never the raw findings array verbatim.

folders.note: FolderAggregates is never referenced anywhere in crates/host/src or crates/summary/src — no folder-level rollup rides the reply. Available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding); unlike nodes/health/recommendations/critical, folders gets not even a compact summary cameo.

gitWindow.note: Forwarded verbatim via a `.get()`-defensive check, only when the key is present (crates/summary/src/analyze/mod.rs) — the facade itself always serializes this field (null when git did not run), but the forwarding step is conditional on presence rather than being an unconditional index. The always-present field is available via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

health.note: Only `health.pain` (the scalar) rides `architecture.pain`; `contributors` (the per-metric breakdown) never reaches the reply. Gated on `health` being non-null (git signals ran this tree). The full health object is available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

ir.note: Deliberate token-shaping decision — the full CommonIr (dep graph, per-file tags/loc/symbols, and `ir.io`'s provide/consume lists) never rides any MCP/`zzop-mcp` reply; it is available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding). An AI consumer gets the io facts it usually wants through the cross-layer join buckets / check_endpoint instead of the raw `ir` block.

layerCoChurn.note: Never referenced anywhere in crates/host/src or crates/summary/src. Available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

nodes.note: The FileNode list is never forwarded (`architecture.criticalTop` derives from `critical`, a separately-ranked view, not from `nodes`). The full per-file node list (tagCounts, loc, every metric) is available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

recommendations.note: Only recommendations[0] (the top ROI-ranked entry) rides `architecture.topRecommendation` ({id, severity, topItem}) — remaining recommendations and each one's full items list are available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

ruleTimings.note: Per-rule performance-timing diagnostic, never referenced in crates/host/src or crates/summary/src. Available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding) — not meant for a curated AI-consumer or human-facing summary.

scores.note: The raw per-metric Scores struct (which feeds health/recommendations) is never forwarded directly — only the DERIVED `health.pain` and top `recommendations[0]` reach the reply via the architecture summary. Raw scores available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

seams.note: SeamCandidate list never referenced in crates/host/src or crates/summary/src. Available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

trees.note: cross_repo reshapes each tree entry into a compact `sources[]` row ({sourceId, path, fileCount, findingCount, packsLoaded, warnings, coverage, ruleOverridesApplied?}) — the raw per-tree AnalyzeOutputView (nodes/scores/health/critical/ir/...) is never forwarded whole; see analyzeOutputView's own rows for what any one nested field gets on its own. The raw per-tree output is available via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

crossLayer.note: cross_repo shapes crossLayer into bucket counts (`buckets.*`), capped edges (`edges`, DEFAULT_EDGES_LIMIT=200, with `edgesTruncated` disclosure), and capped distinct bucket keys (`bucketKeys`/`bucketKeySites`, DEFAULT_BUCKET_KEYS_LIMIT=20, with `bucketKeysTruncated`) — never the raw CrossLayerResult object verbatim. The raw edges/unprovidedConsumes/unconsumedProvides/unresolvedConsumes/externalConsumes/ambiguousConsumes buckets are available only via the raw zzop-facade output (direct engine embedding).

crossLayerFindings.note: Shaped via output::shape_findings identically to per-tree findings (total/bySeverity/byRule/shown+truncated) — never the raw array verbatim.

warnings.note: Forwarded to `summary.warnings` only when the top-level key is present AND non-empty (`v.get("warnings")` in the cross summary handler) — unlike the always-serialized per-tree warnings/configWarnings fields, this run-level channel is presence-gated, not an unconditional index. History: this row was the registry's first catch — when first authored, no delivery surface read the run-level field at all (the parallel-implementation-tripwire self-report was silently dropped), the exact shape this registry exists to prevent.
